text.NRSV: A great portent appeared in heaven: a woman clothed with the sun, with the moon under her feet, and on her head a crown of twelve stars.
text.DRB: And a great sign appeared in heaven; a woman clothed with the sun, and the moon under her feet, & on her head a crown of twelve stars:
text.Rotherham: And, a great sign, appeared in heaven: a woman arrayed with the sun, and, the moon, beneath her feet, and, upon her head, a crown of twelve stars;

text.KJV: And she being with child cried, travailing in birth, and pained to be delivered.
text.NABRE: She was with child and wailed aloud in pain as she labored to give birth.
text.NRSV: She was pregnant and was crying out in birth pangs, in the agony of giving birth.
text.DRB: and being with child, she cried also travailing, and is in anguish to be delivered.
text.Rotherham: and she was with child, and crieth out, being in pangs and in anguish to bring forth?

text.KJV: And there appeared another wonder in heaven; and behold a great red dragon, having seven heads and ten horns, and seven crowns upon his heads.
text.NABRE: Then another sign appeared in the sky; it was a huge red dragon, with seven heads and ten horns, and on its heads were seven diadems.
text.NRSV: Then another portent appeared in heaven: a great red dragon, with seven heads and ten horns, and seven diadems on his heads.
text.DRB: And there was seen another sign in heaven, and behold a great red dragon having seven heads, & ten horns: and on his heads seven diadems,
text.Rotherham: And there appeared another sign in heaven; and lo! a great red dragon,—having seven heads and ten horns, and, upon his heads, seven diadems;

text.KJV: And his tail drew the third part of the stars of heaven, and did cast them to the earth: and the dragon stood before the woman which was ready to be delivered, for to devour her child as soon as it was born.
text.NABRE: Its tail swept away a third of the stars in the sky and hurled them down to the earth. Then the dragon stood before the woman about to give birth, to devour her child when she gave birth.
text.NRSV: His tail swept down a third of the stars of heaven and threw them to the earth. Then the dragon stood before the woman who was about to bear a child, so that he might devour her child as soon as it was born.
text.DRB: and his tail drew the third part of the stars of heaven, and cast them to the earth: and the dragon stood before the woman which was ready to be delivered; that when she should be delivered, he might devour her son.
text.Rotherham: and, his tail, draweth the third part of the stars of heaven, and did cast them to the earth. And, the dragon, stood before the woman who was about to bring forth, that, as soon as she should bring forth, he might devour, her child.

text.KJV: And she brought forth a man child, who was to rule all nations with a rod of iron: and her child was caught up unto God, and to his throne.
text.NABRE: She gave birth to a son, a male child, destined to rule all the nations with an iron rod. Her child was caught up to God and his throne.
text.NRSV: And she gave birth to a son, a male child, who is to rule all the nations with a rod of iron. But her child was snatched away and taken to God and to his throne;
text.DRB: And she brought forth a man child, who was to govern all nations in an iron rod; & her son was taken up to God and to his throne,
text.Rotherham: And she brought forth a son, a manchild, who was about to shepherd all the nations with a sceptre of iron; and her child was caught away unto God and unto his throne.

text.KJV: And the woman fled into the wilderness, where she hath a place prepared of God, that they should feed her there a thousand two hundred and threescore days.
text.NABRE: The woman herself fled into the desert where she had a place prepared by God, that there she might be taken care of for twelve hundred and sixty days.
text.NRSV: and the woman fled into the wilderness, where she has a place prepared by God, so that there she can be nourished for one thousand two hundred sixty days.
text.DRB: & the woman fled into the wilderness where she had a place prepared of God, that there they might feed her a thousand two hundred sixty days.
text.Rotherham: And, the woman, fled into the desert, where she hath a place prepared of God, that, there, they should nourish her a thousand, two hundred, and sixty days.

text.KJV: And there was war in heaven: Michael and his angels fought against the dragon; and the dragon fought and his angels,
text.NABRE: Then war broke out in heaven; Michael and his angels battled against the dragon. The dragon and its angels fought back,
text.NRSV: And war broke out in heaven; Michael and his angels fought against the dragon. The dragon and his angels fought back,
text.DRB: And there was made a great battle in heaven, Michael and his Angels fought with the dragon, and the dragon fought and his Angels:
text.Rotherham: And there came to be war in heaven: Michael and his messengers [going forth] to war with the dragon; and, the dragon, fought, and his messengers;

text.KJV: And prevailed not; neither was their place found any more in heaven.
text.NABRE: but they did not prevail and there was no longer any place for them in heaven.
text.NRSV: but they were defeated, and there was no longer any place for them in heaven.
text.DRB: and they prevailed not, neither was their place found any more in heaven.
text.Rotherham: and he prevailed not, neither was place found for them, any longer, in heaven.

text.KJV: And the great dragon was cast out, that old serpent, called the Devil, and Satan, which deceiveth the whole world: he was cast out into the earth, and his angels were cast out with him.
text.NABRE: The huge dragon, the ancient serpent, who is called the Devil and Satan, who deceived the whole world, was thrown down to earth, and its angels were thrown down with it.
text.NRSV: The great dragon was thrown down, that ancient serpent, who is called the Devil and Satan, the deceiver of the whole world—he was thrown down to the earth, and his angels were thrown down with him.
text.DRB: And that great dragon was cast forth, the old serpent, which is called the devil and Satan, which seduceth the whole world: and he was cast into the earth, & his Angels were thrown down with him.
text.Rotherham: And the great dragon was cast out,—the ancient serpent, he that is called Adversary and the Satan, that deceiveth the whole habitable world,—he was cast to the earth, and his messengers, with him, were cast.

text.KJV: And I heard a loud voice saying in heaven, Now is come salvation, and strength, and the kingdom of our God, and the power of his Christ: for the accuser of our brethren is cast down, which accused them before our God day and night.
text.NABRE: Then I heard a loud voice in heaven say: “Now have salvation and power come, and the kingdom of our God and the authority of his Anointed. For the accuser of our brothers is cast out, who accuses them before our God day and night.
text.NRSV: Then I heard a loud voice in heaven, proclaiming, “Now have come the salvation and the power and the kingdom of our God and the authority of his Messiah, for the accuser of our comrades has been thrown down, who accuses them day and night before our God.
text.DRB: And I heard a great voice in heaven saying: Now is there made salvation and force, and the Kingdom of our God, and the power of his Christ: because the accuser of our brethren is cast forth, who accused them before the sight of our God day and night.
text.Rotherham: And I heard a loud voice in heaven, saying—Now, hath come the salvation, and the power, and the kingdom of our God, and the authority of his Christ; because the accuser of our brethren hath been cast out, who was accusing them before our God day and night;

text.KJV: And they overcame him by the blood of the Lamb, and by the word of their testimony; and they loved not their lives unto the death.
text.NABRE: They conquered him by the blood of the Lamb and by the word of their testimony; love for life did not deter them from death.
text.NRSV: But they have conquered him by the blood of the Lamb and by the word of their testimony, for they did not cling to life even in the face of death.
text.DRB: And they overcame him by the blood of the Lamb, and by the word of their testimony, and they loved not their lives even unto death.
text.Rotherham: And, they, overcame him by reason of the blood of the Lamb, and by reason of their witnessing word, and they loved not their life, even unto death.

text.KJV: Therefore rejoice, ye heavens, and ye that dwell in them. Woe to the inhabiters of the earth and of the sea! for the devil is come down unto you, having great wrath, because he knoweth that he hath but a short time.
text.NABRE: Therefore, rejoice, you heavens, and you who dwell in them. But woe to you, earth and sea, for the Devil has come down to you in great fury, for he knows he has but a short time.”
text.NRSV: Rejoice then, you heavens and those who dwell in them! But woe to the earth and the sea, for the devil has come down to you with great wrath, because he knows that his time is short!”
text.DRB: Therefore rejoice, o heavens, and you that dwell therein. Woe to the earth & to the sea, because the devil is descended to you, having great wrath, knowing that he hath a little time.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, be joyful, O heavens, and ye who, therein, are tabernacling. Woe! unto the earth, and the sea, because the Adversary hath come down unto you, having great wrath—knowing that, but a little season, he hath.

text.KJV: And when the dragon saw that he was cast unto the earth, he persecuted the woman which brought forth the man child.
text.NABRE: When the dragon saw that it had been thrown down to the earth, it pursued the woman who had given birth to the male child.
text.NRSV: So when the dragon saw that he had been thrown down to the earth, he pursued the woman who had given birth to the male child.
text.DRB: And after the dragon saw that he was thrown into the earth, he persecuted the woman which brought forth the man-child:
text.Rotherham: And, when the dragon saw that he was cast to the earth, he pursued the woman who had brought forth the manchild.

text.KJV: And to the woman were given two wings of a great eagle, that she might fly into the wilderness, into her place, where she is nourished for a time, and times, and half a time, from the face of the serpent.
text.NABRE: But the woman was given the two wings of the great eagle, so that she could fly to her place in the desert, where, far from the serpent, she was taken care of for a year, two years, and a half-year.
text.NRSV: But the woman was given the two wings of the great eagle, so that she could fly from the serpent into the wilderness, to her place where she is nourished for a time, and times, and half a time.
text.DRB: and there were given to the woman two wings of a great eagle, that she might fly into the desert unto her place, where she is nourished for a time & times, & half a time, from the face of the serpent.
text.Rotherham: And there were given unto the woman the two wings of the great eagle, that she might fly into the desert, into her place,—where she is nourished, a season and seasons and half a season, from the face of the serpent.

text.KJV: And the serpent cast out of his mouth water as a flood after the woman, that he might cause her to be carried away of the flood.
text.NABRE: The serpent, however, spewed a torrent of water out of his mouth after the woman to sweep her away with the current.
text.NRSV: Then from his mouth the serpent poured water like a river after the woman, to sweep her away with the flood.
text.DRB: And the serpent cast out of his mouth after the woman, water as it were a flood: that he might make her to be carried away with the flood.
text.Rotherham: And the serpent cast out of his mouth, after the woman, water as a river, that he might cause her to be carried away by the stream.

text.KJV: And the earth helped the woman, and the earth opened her mouth, and swallowed up the flood which the dragon cast out of his mouth.
text.NABRE: But the earth helped the woman and opened its mouth and swallowed the flood that the dragon spewed out of its mouth.
text.NRSV: But the earth came to the help of the woman; it opened its mouth and swallowed the river that the dragon had poured from his mouth.
text.DRB: And the earth holp the woman, and the earth opened her mouth, and swallowed up the flood which the dragon cast out of his mouth.
text.Rotherham: And the earth helped the woman, and the earth opened her mouth, and swallowed up the river which the dragon cast out of his mouth.

text.KJV: And the dragon was wroth with the woman, and went to make war with the remnant of her seed, which keep the commandments of God, and have the testimony of Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: Then the dragon became angry with the woman and went off to wage war against the rest of her offspring, those who keep God’s commandments and bear witness to Jesus.
text.NRSV: Then the dragon was angry with the woman, and went off to make war on the rest of her children, those who keep the commandments of God and hold the testimony of Jesus.
text.DRB: And the dragon was angry against the woman: and went to make battle with the rest of her seed, which keep the commandments of God, and have the testimony of Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: And the dragon was angered against the woman, and went away to make war with the rest of her seed—with them who were keeping the commandments of God, and holding the witness of Jesus;—and he stood upon the sand of the sea.

text.NABRE: It took its position on the sand of the sea.
text.NRSV: Then the dragon took his stand on the sand of the seashore.
text.DRB: And he stood upon the sand of the sea.
text.KJV: And I stood upon the sand of the sea,

text.NRSV: And I saw a beast rising out of the sea, having ten horns and seven heads; and on its horns were ten diadems, and on its heads were blasphemous names.
text.Rotherham: And I saw, out of the sea, a wild-beast coming up; having ten horns, and seven heads, and, upon his horns, ten diadems, and, upon his head, names of blasphemy.
text.KJV: And I saw a beast rise up out of the sea, having seven heads and ten horns, and upon his horns ten crowns, and upon his heads the name of blasphemy.
text.NABRE: Chapter 13 The First Beast. Then I saw a beast come out of the sea with ten horns and seven heads; on its horns were ten diadems, and on its heads blasphemous name[s].
text.DRB: And I saw a beast coming up from the sea, having seven heads, and ten horns, & upon his horns ten diadems, and upon his heads names of blasphemy.

text.NRSV: And the beast that I saw was like a leopard, its feet were like a bear’s, and its mouth was like a lion’s mouth. And the dragon gave it his power and his throne and great authority.
text.Rotherham: And, the beast which I saw, was like unto a leopard; and, his feet, as of a bear, and, his mouth, as the mouth of a lion. And the dragon gave unto him his power, and his throne, and great authority.
text.KJV: And the beast which I saw was like unto a leopard, and his feet were as the feet of a bear, and his mouth as the mouth of a lion: and the dragon gave him his power, and his seat, and great authority.
text.NABRE: The beast I saw was like a leopard, but it had feet like a bear’s, and its mouth was like the mouth of a lion. To it the dragon gave its own power and throne, along with great authority.
text.DRB: And the beast which I saw, was like to a libard, and his feet as of a bear, and his mouth, as the mouth of a lion. And the dragon gave him his own force and great power.

text.NRSV: One of its heads seemed to have received a death-blow, but its mortal wound was healed. In amazement the whole earth followed the beast.
text.Rotherham: And [I saw] one of his heads, showing that it had been slain unto death, and, the stroke of his death, was healed. And the whole earth marveled after the wild-beast,
text.KJV: And I saw one of his heads as it were wounded to death; and his deadly wound was healed: and all the world wondered after the beast.
text.NABRE: I saw that one of its heads seemed to have been mortally wounded, but this mortal wound was healed. Fascinated, the whole world followed after the beast.
text.DRB: And I saw one of his heads as it were slain to death: and the wound of his death was cured. And all the earth was in admiration after the beast.

text.NRSV: They worshiped the dragon, for he had given his authority to the beast, and they worshiped the beast, saying, “Who is like the beast, and who can fight against it?”
text.Rotherham: and did homage unto the dragon, because he gave his authority unto the wild-beast; and they did homage unto the wild-beast, saying—Who is like unto the wild-beast? and Who can make war with him?
text.KJV: And they worshipped the dragon which gave power unto the beast: and they worshipped the beast, saying, Who is like unto the beast? who is able to make war with him?
text.NABRE: They worshiped the dragon because it gave its authority to the beast; they also worshiped the beast and said, “Who can compare with the beast or who can fight against it?”
text.DRB: And they adored the dragon which gave power to the beast: and they adored the beast, saying: Who is like to the beast? and who shall be able to fight with it?

text.NRSV: The beast was given a mouth uttering haughty and blasphemous words, and it was allowed to exercise authority for forty-two months.
text.Rotherham: And there was given unto him, a mouth speaking great things and blasphemies; and it was given unto him to act, forty and two months.
text.KJV: And there was given unto him a mouth speaking great things and blasphemies; and power was given unto him to continue forty and two months.
text.NABRE: The beast was given a mouth uttering proud boasts and blasphemies, and it was given authority to act for forty-two months.
text.DRB: And there was given to it a mouth speaking great things and blasphemies: and power was given to it to work two and forty months.

text.NRSV: It opened its mouth to utter blasphemies against God, blaspheming his name and his dwelling, that is, those who dwell in heaven.
text.Rotherham: And he opened his mouth for blasphemy against God, to blaspheme his name, and his tent,—them, who, in heaven were tabernacling.
text.KJV: And he opened his mouth in blasphemy against God, to blaspheme his name, and his tabernacle, and them that dwell in heaven.
text.NABRE: It opened its mouth to utter blasphemies against God, blaspheming his name and his dwelling and those who dwell in heaven.
text.DRB: And he opened his mouth unto blasphemies toward God, to blaspheme his name, & his tabernacle, & those that dwell in heaven.

text.NRSV: Also it was allowed to make war on the saints and to conquer them. It was given authority over every tribe and people and language and nation,
text.Rotherham: [And it was given unto him, to make war with the saints, and to overcome them;] and there was given him, authority against every tribe and people and tongue and nation.
text.KJV: And it was given unto him to make war with the saints, and to overcome them: and power was given him over all kindreds, and tongues, and nations.
text.NABRE: It was also allowed to wage war against the holy ones and conquer them, and it was granted authority over every tribe, people, tongue, and nation.
text.DRB: And it was given unto him to make battail with the saints, & to overcome them. And power was given him upon every tribe and people, and tongue, and nation,

text.NRSV: and all the inhabitants of the earth will worship it, everyone whose name has not been written from the foundation of the world in the book of life of the Lamb that was slaughtered.
text.Rotherham: And all they who are dwelling upon the earth will do homage unto him,—[every one] whose name is not written in the scroll of life of the Lamb slain from the foundation of the world.
text.KJV: And all that dwell upon the earth shall worship him, whose names are not written in the book of life of the Lamb slain from the foundation of the world.
text.NABRE: All the inhabitants of the earth will worship it, all whose names were not written from the foundation of the world in the book of life, which belongs to the Lamb who was slain.
text.DRB: and all that inhabit the earth, adored it, whose names be not written in the book of life of the Lamb, which was slain from the beginning of the world.

text.NRSV: Let anyone who has an ear listen:
text.Rotherham: If anyone hath an ear: let him hear.
text.KJV: If any man have an ear, let him hear.
text.NABRE: Whoever has ears ought to hear these words.
text.DRB: If any man have an ear, let him hear.

text.NRSV: If you are to be taken captive, into captivity you go; if you are to be killed by the sword, with the sword you must be killed. Here is a call for the endurance and faith of the saints.
text.Rotherham: If anyone [carrieth] into captivity, into captivity, he goeth away. If anyone, with a sword, doth slay, he must, with a sword, be slain. Here, is the endurance and the faith of the saints.
text.KJV: He that leadeth into captivity shall go into captivity: he that killeth with the sword must be killed with the sword. Here is the patience and the faith of the saints.
text.NABRE: Anyone destined for captivity goes into captivity. Anyone destined to be slain by the sword shall be slain by the sword. Such is the faithful endurance of the holy ones. The Second Beast.
text.DRB: He that shall lead into captivity, goeth into captivity: he that shall kill in the sword, he must be killed with the sword. Here is the patience and the faith of saints.

text.NRSV: Then I saw another beast that rose out of the earth; it had two horns like a lamb and it spoke like a dragon.
text.Rotherham: And I saw another wild-beast, coming up out of the earth; and he had two horns, like unto a lamb, and began speaking as a dragon.
text.KJV: And I beheld another beast coming up out of the earth; and he had two horns like a lamb, and he spake as a dragon.
text.NABRE: Then I saw another beast come up out of the earth; it had two horns like a lamb’s but spoke like a dragon.
text.DRB: And I saw another beast coming up from the earth: and he had two horns, like to a lamb, & he spake as a dragon.

text.NRSV: It exercises all the authority of the first beast on its behalf, and it makes the earth and its inhabitants worship the first beast, whose mortal wound had been healed.
text.Rotherham: And, all the authority of the first wild-beast, he useth before him; and causeth the earth, and them who, therein, are dwelling, that they shall do homage unto the first wild-beast, whose stroke of death, was healed.
text.KJV: And he exerciseth all the power of the first beast before him, and causeth the earth and them which dwell therein to worship the first beast, whose deadly wound was healed.
text.NABRE: It wielded all the authority of the first beast in its sight and made the earth and its inhabitants worship the first beast, whose mortal wound had been healed.
text.DRB: And all the power of the former beast he did in his sight: and he made the earth and the inhabitants therein, to adore the first beast, whose wound of death was cured.

text.NRSV: It performs great signs, even making fire come down from heaven to earth in the sight of all;
text.Rotherham: And he doeth great signs, so that, even fire, he causeth to be coming down unto the earth before men;
text.KJV: And he doeth great wonders, so that he maketh fire come down from heaven on the earth in the sight of men,
text.NABRE: It performed great signs, even making fire come down from heaven to earth in the sight of everyone.
text.DRB: And he did many signs, so that he made also fire to come down from heaven unto the earth in the sight of men.

text.NRSV: and by the signs that it is allowed to perform on behalf of the beast, it deceives the inhabitants of earth, telling them to make an image for the beast that had been wounded by the sword and yet lived;
text.Rotherham: and he deceiveth them who are dwelling upon the earth, by reason of the signs which it was given him to do before the wild-beast,—saying unto them who are dwelling upon the earth, that they should make an image unto the wild-beast who hath the stroke of the sword, and yet did live.
text.KJV: And deceiveth them that dwell on the earth by the means of those miracles which he had power to do in the sight of the beast; saying to them that dwell on the earth, that they should make an image to the beast, which had the wound by a sword, and did live.
text.NABRE: It deceived the inhabitants of the earth with the signs it was allowed to perform in the sight of the first beast, telling them to make an image for the beast who had been wounded by the sword and revived.
text.DRB: And he seduceth the inhabitants on the earth through the signs which were given him to do in the sight of the beast, saying to them that dwell on the earth, that they should make the image of the beast which hath the stroke of the sword, and lived.

text.NRSV: and it was allowed to give breath to the image of the beast so that the image of the beast could even speak and cause those who would not worship the image of the beast to be killed.
text.Rotherham: And it was given unto it, to give spirit unto the image of the wild-beast, in order that the image of the wild-beast should both speak and should cause that, as many as should not do homage unto the image of the wild-beast, should be slain.
text.KJV: And he had power to give life unto the image of the beast, that the image of the beast should both speak, and cause that as many as would not worship the image of the beast should be killed.
text.NABRE: It was then permitted to breathe life into the beast’s image, so that the beast’s image could speak and [could] have anyone who did not worship it put to death.
text.DRB: And it was given him to give spirit to the image of the beast, and that the image of the beast should speak: and should make, that whosoever shall not adore the image of the beast, be slain.

text.NRSV: Also it causes all, both small and great, both rich and poor, both free and slave, to be marked on the right hand or the forehead,
text.Rotherham: And he causeth all—the small and the great, and the rich and the poor, and the free and the bond,—that they should give unto them a mark, upon their right hand or upon their forehead;
text.KJV: And he causeth all, both small and great, rich and poor, free and bond, to receive a mark in their right hand, or in their foreheads:
text.NABRE: It forced all the people, small and great, rich and poor, free and slave, to be given a stamped image on their right hands or their foreheads,
text.DRB: And he shall make all, little & great, and rich, and poor, and free-men, and bond-men, to have a character in their right hand, or in their foreheads.

text.NRSV: so that no one can buy or sell who does not have the mark, that is, the name of the beast or the number of its name.
text.Rotherham: [and] that no one should be able to buy or to sell, save he that hath the mark, the name of the beast, or the number of his name.
text.KJV: And that no man might buy or sell, save he that had the mark, or the name of the beast, or the number of his name.
text.NABRE: so that no one could buy or sell except one who had the stamped image of the beast’s name or the number that stood for its name.
text.DRB: And that no man may buy or sell, but he that hath the character, or the name of the beast, or the number of his name.

text.NRSV: This calls for wisdom: let anyone with understanding calculate the number of the beast, for it is the number of a person. Its number is six hundred sixty-six.
text.Rotherham: Here, is, wisdom: he that hath understanding, let him count the number of the beast, for it is, the number of a man; and, his number, is 666.
text.KJV: Here is wisdom. Let him that hath understanding count the number of the beast: for it is the number of a man; and his number is Six hundred threescore and six.
text.NABRE: Wisdom is needed here; one who understands can calculate the number of the beast, for it is a number that stands for a person. His number is six hundred and sixty-six.
text.DRB: Here is wisdom. He that hath understanding, let him count the number of the beast. For it is the number of a man; and the number of him is six hundred sixty six.

text.NRSV: Then I looked, and there was the Lamb, standing on Mount Zion! And with him were one hundred forty-four thousand who had his name and his Father’s name written on their foreheads.
text.Rotherham: And I saw, and lo! the Lamb, standing upon the mount Zion,—and, with him, a hundred and forty-four thousand, having his name and his Father’s name written upon their foreheads.
text.KJV: And I looked, and, lo, a Lamb stood on the mount Sion, and with him an hundred forty and four thousand, having his Father’s name written in their foreheads.
text.NABRE: Chapter 14 The Lamb’s Companions. Then I looked and there was the Lamb standing on Mount Zion, and with him a hundred and forty-four thousand who had his name and his Father’s name written on their foreheads.
text.DRB: And I looked, & behold a Lamb stood upon mount Sion, & with him an hundred forty four thousand having his name, and the name of his Father written in their foreheads.

text.NRSV: And I heard a voice from heaven like the sound of many waters and like the sound of loud thunder; the voice I heard was like the sound of harpists playing on their harps,
text.Rotherham: And I heard a sound out of heaven, as the sound of many waters, and as the sound of, loud thunders; and, the sound which I heard, was as of harp-singers harping with their harps,
text.KJV: And I heard a voice from heaven, as the voice of many waters, and as the voice of a great thunder: and I heard the voice of harpers harping with their harps:
text.NABRE: I heard a sound from heaven like the sound of rushing water or a loud peal of thunder. The sound I heard was like that of harpists playing their harps.
text.DRB: And I heard a voice from heaven, as the voice of many waters, and as the voice of great thunder: and the voice which I heard, as of harpers harping on their harps.

text.NRSV: and they sing a new song before the throne and before the four living creatures and before the elders. No one could learn that song except the one hundred forty-four thousand who have been redeemed from the earth.
text.Rotherham: And they sing as it were a new song before the throne, and before the four living creatures and the elders. And, no one, was able to learn the song, save the hundred and forty-four thousand, who had been redeemed from the earth.
text.KJV: And they sung as it were a new song before the throne, and before the four beasts, and the elders: and no man could learn that song but the hundred and forty and four thousand, which were redeemed from the earth.
text.NABRE: They were singing [what seemed to be] a new hymn before the throne, before the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn this hymn except the hundred and forty-four thousand who had been ransomed from the earth.
text.DRB: And they sang as it were a new song before the seat and before the four beasts, and the Seniors, & no man could say the song, but those hundred forty four thousand, that were bought from the earth.

text.NRSV: It is these who have not defiled themselves with women, for they are virgins; these follow the Lamb wherever he goes. They have been redeemed from humankind as first fruits for God and the Lamb,
text.Rotherham: These, are they, who with women, were not defiled, for they are, virgin. These, are they who follow the Lamb whithersoever he is going. These, were redeemed from mankind, as a firstfruit unto God and the Lamb;
text.KJV: These are they which were not defiled with women; for they are virgins. These are they which follow the Lamb whithersoever he goeth. These were redeemed from among men, being the firstfruits unto God and to the Lamb.
text.NABRE: These are they who were not defiled with women; they are virgins and these are the ones who follow the Lamb wherever he goes. They have been ransomed as the firstfruits of the human race for God and the Lamb.
text.DRB: These are they which were not defiled with women. For they are virgins. These follow the Lamb whithersoever he shall go. These were bought from among men, the first fruits to God and the Lamb:

text.NRSV: and in their mouth no lie was found; they are blameless.
text.Rotherham: and, in their mouth, was found no falsehood,—faultless, they are.
text.KJV: And in their mouth was found no guile: for they are without fault before the throne of God.
text.NABRE: On their lips no deceit has been found; they are unblemished. The Three Angels.
text.DRB: and in their mouth there was found no lie. For they are without spot before the throne of God.

text.NRSV: Then I saw another angel flying in midheaven, with an eternal gospel to proclaim to those who live on the earth—to every nation and tribe and language and people;
text.Rotherham: And I saw another messenger, flying in mid-heaven, having an age-abiding glad-message to announce unto them who are dwelling upon the earth, even unto every nation and tribe and tongue and people,
text.KJV: And I saw another angel fly in the midst of heaven, having the everlasting gospel to preach unto them that dwell on the earth, and to every nation, and kindred, and tongue, and people,
text.NABRE: Then I saw another angel flying high overhead, with everlasting good news to announce to those who dwell on earth, to every nation, tribe, tongue, and people.
text.DRB: And I saw another Angel flying through the midst of heaven, having the eternal Gospel, to evangelize unto them that sit upon the earth, and upon every nation, and tribe, and tongue, and people;

text.NRSV: he said in a loud voice, “Fear God and give him glory, for the hour of his judgment has come; and worship him who made heaven and earth, the sea and the springs of water.”
text.Rotherham: saying with a loud voice,—Fear God and give him glory, because the hour of his judging is come; and do homage unto him that made heaven and the earth and sea and fountains of waters.
text.KJV: Saying with a loud voice, Fear God, and give glory to him; for the hour of his judgment is come: and worship him that made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and the fountains of waters.
text.NABRE: He said in a loud voice, “Fear God and give him glory, for his time has come to sit in judgment. Worship him who made heaven and earth and sea and springs of water.”
text.DRB: saying with a loud voice: Fear our Lord, and give him honour, because the hour of his judgement is come: and adore ye him that made heaven and earth, the sea and all things that are in them, and the fountains of waters.

text.NRSV: Then another angel, a second, followed, saying, “Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She has made all nations drink of the wine of the wrath of her fornication.”
text.Rotherham: And, another, a second [messenger] followed, saying—Fallen! fallen! is Babylon the great, who, of the wine of the wrath of her lewdness, hath caused all the nations to drink.
text.KJV: And there followed another angel, saying, Babylon is fallen, is fallen, that great city, because she made all nations drink of the wine of the wrath of her fornication.
text.NABRE: A second angel followed, saying: “Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great, that made all the nations drink the wine of her licentious passion.”
text.DRB: And another Angel followed, saying: Fallen fallen is that great Babylon, which of the wine of the wrath of her fornication made all nations to drink.

text.NRSV: Then another angel, a third, followed them, crying with a loud voice, “Those who worship the beast and its image, and receive a mark on their foreheads or on their hands,
text.Rotherham: And, another, a third messenger, followed them, saying with a loud voice—If anyone doeth homage unto the beast and his image, and receiveth a mark upon his forehead, or upon his hand,
text.KJV: And the third angel followed them, saying with a loud voice, If any man worship the beast and his image, and receive his mark in his forehead, or in his hand,
text.NABRE: A third angel followed them and said in a loud voice, “Anyone who worships the beast or its image, or accepts its mark on forehead or hand,
text.DRB: And the third Angel followed them, saying with a loud voice: If any man adore the beast and his image, and receive the character in his forehead, or in his hand;

text.NRSV: they will also drink the wine of God’s wrath, poured unmixed into the cup of his anger, and they will be tormented with fire and sulfur in the presence of the holy angels and in the presence of the Lamb.
text.Rotherham: he also, shall drink of the wine of the wrath of God, which is prepared, unmixed, in the cup of his anger;—and he shall be tormented with fire and brimstone, before holy messengers and before the Lamb;
text.KJV: The same shall drink of the wine of the wrath of God, which is poured out without mixture into the cup of his indignation; and he shall be tormented with fire and brimstone in the presence of the holy angels, and in the presence of the Lamb:
text.NABRE: will also drink the wine of God’s fury, poured full strength into the cup of his wrath, and will be tormented in burning sulfur before the holy angels and before the Lamb.
text.DRB: he also shall drink of the wine of the wrath of God, which is mingled with pure wine in the cup of his wrath, and shall be tormented with fire & brimstone in the sight of the holy Angels and before the sight of the Lamb.

text.NRSV: And the smoke of their torment goes up forever and ever. There is no rest day or night for those who worship the beast and its image and for anyone who receives the mark of its name.”
text.Rotherham: And, the smoke of their torment, unto ages of ages, ascendeth; And they have no rest day or night, who do homage unto the beast and his image, or if anyone receiveth the mark of his name.
text.KJV: And the smoke of their torment ascendeth up for ever and ever: and they have no rest day nor night, who worship the beast and his image, and whosoever receiveth the mark of his name.
text.NABRE: The smoke of the fire that torments them will rise forever and ever, and there will be no relief day or night for those who worship the beast or its image or accept the mark of its name.”
text.DRB: And the smoke of their torments shall ascend for ever and ever: neither have they rest day and night, which have adored the beast, and his image, and if any man take the character of his name.

text.NRSV: Here is a call for the endurance of the saints, those who keep the commandments of God and hold fast to the faith of Jesus.
text.Rotherham: Here, is, the endurance of the saints,—they who keep the commandments of God and the faith of Jesus.
text.KJV: Here is the patience of the saints: here are they that keep the commandments of God, and the faith of Jesus.
text.NABRE: Here is what sustains the holy ones who keep God’s commandments and their faith in Jesus.
text.DRB: Here is the patience of saints, which keep the commandments of God and the faith of Jesus.

text.NRSV: And I heard a voice from heaven saying, “Write this: Blessed are the dead who from now on die in the Lord.” “Yes,” says the Spirit, “they will rest from their labors, for their deeds follow them.”
text.Rotherham: And I heard a voice out of heaven, saying—Write! Happy, the dead who, in the Lord, do die, from henceforth. Yea! (saith the Spirit) that they may rest from their toils, for, their works, do follow with them.
text.KJV: And I heard a voice from heaven saying unto me, Write, Blessed are the dead which die in the Lord from henceforth: Yea, saith the Spirit, that they may rest from their labours; and their works do follow them.
text.NABRE: I heard a voice from heaven say, “Write this: Blessed are the dead who die in the Lord from now on.” “Yes,” said the Spirit, “let them find rest from their labors, for their works accompany them.” The Harvest of the Earth.
text.DRB: And I heard a voice from heaven, saying to me: Write, Blessed are the dead which die in our Lord. From hence-forth now, saith the Spirit, that they rest from their labours. For their works follow them.

text.NRSV: Then I looked, and there was a white cloud, and seated on the cloud was one like the Son of Man, with a golden crown on his head, and a sharp sickle in his hand!
text.Rotherham: And I saw, and lo! a white cloud, and, upon the cloud, one sitting like unto a son of man, having, upon his head, a crown of gold, and, in his hand, a sharp sickle.
text.KJV: And I looked, and behold a white cloud, and upon the cloud one sat like unto the Son of man, having on his head a golden crown, and in his hand a sharp sickle.
text.NABRE: Then I looked and there was a white cloud, and sitting on the cloud one who looked like a son of man, with a gold crown on his head and a sharp sickle in his hand.
text.DRB: And I saw, and behold a white cloud: and upon the cloud one sitting like to the Son of man, having on his head a crown of gold, and in his hand a sharp sickle.

text.NRSV: Another angel came out of the temple, calling with a loud voice to the one who sat on the cloud, “Use your sickle and reap, for the hour to reap has come, because the harvest of the earth is fully ripe.”
text.Rotherham: And, another messenger, came forth out of the sanctuary, crying out with a loud voice, unto him that was sitting upon the cloud—Thrust in thy sickle, and reap; because the hour to reap is come, because the harvest of the earth is ripe.
text.KJV: And another angel came out of the temple, crying with a loud voice to him that sat on the cloud, Thrust in thy sickle, and reap: for the time is come for thee to reap; for the harvest of the earth is ripe.
text.NABRE: Another angel came out of the temple, crying out in a loud voice to the one sitting on the cloud, “Use your sickle and reap the harvest, for the time to reap has come, because the earth’s harvest is fully ripe.”
text.DRB: And another Angel came forth from the temple, crying with a loud voice to him that sat upon the cloud: Thrust in thy sickle, and reap, because the hour is come to reap, for the harvest of the earth is dry.

text.NRSV: So the one who sat on the cloud swung his sickle over the earth, and the earth was reaped.
text.Rotherham: And he that was sitting upon the cloud, thrust in, his sickle upon the earth; and the earth was reaped.
text.KJV: And he that sat on the cloud thrust in his sickle on the earth; and the earth was reaped.
text.NABRE: So the one who was sitting on the cloud swung his sickle over the earth, and the earth was harvested.
text.DRB: And he that sat upon the cloud, thrust his sickle into the earth, and the earth was reaped.

text.NRSV: Then another angel came out of the temple in heaven, and he too had a sharp sickle.
text.Rotherham: And, another messenger, came forth out of the sanctuary that is in heaven,—he also, having a sharp sickle.
text.KJV: And another angel came out of the temple which is in heaven, he also having a sharp sickle.
text.NABRE: Then another angel came out of the temple in heaven who also had a sharp sickle.
text.DRB: And another Angel came forth from the temple which is in heaven, himself also having a sharp sickle.

text.NRSV: Then another angel came out from the altar, the angel who has authority over fire, and he called with a loud voice to the one who had the sharp sickle, “Use your sharp sickle and gather the clusters of the vine of the earth, for its grapes are ripe.”
text.Rotherham: And, another messenger, [came forth] out of the altar, who hath authority over the fire,—and called out with a loud voice, unto him who had the sharp sickle, saying—Thrust in thy sharp sickle, and gather the clusters of the vine of the earth; because the grapes thereof are fully ripe.
text.KJV: And another angel came out from the altar, which had power over fire; and cried with a loud cry to him that had the sharp sickle, saying, Thrust in thy sharp sickle, and gather the clusters of the vine of the earth; for her grapes are fully ripe.
text.NABRE: Then another angel [came] from the altar, [who] was in charge of the fire, and cried out in a loud voice to the one who had the sharp sickle, “Use your sharp sickle and cut the clusters from the earth’s vines, for its grapes are ripe.”
text.DRB: And another Angel came forth from the altar, which had power over the fire: and he cried with a loud voice to him that had the sharp sickle, saying: Thrust in thy sharp sickle, and gather the clusters of the vineyard of the earth: because the grapes thereof be ripe.

text.NRSV: So the angel swung his sickle over the earth and gathered the vintage of the earth, and he threw it into the great winepress of the wrath of God.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger, thrust in, his sickle into the earth, and gathered the vine of the earth, and cast [it] into the great wine-press of the wrath of God.
text.KJV: And the angel thrust in his sickle into the earth, and gathered the vine of the earth, and cast it into the great winepress of the wrath of God.
text.NABRE: So the angel swung his sickle over the earth and cut the earth’s vintage. He threw it into the great wine press of God’s fury.
text.DRB: And the Angel thrust his sharp sickle into the earth, and gathered the vineyard of the earth, & cast it into the great press of the wrath of God:

text.NRSV: And the winepress was trodden outside the city, and blood flowed from the winepress, as high as a horse’s bridle, for a distance of about two hundred miles.
text.Rotherham: And the wine-press was trodden outside the city, and there came forth blood out of the wine-press, even unto the bits of the horses, at a distance of a thousand six hundred furlongs.
text.KJV: And the winepress was trodden without the city, and blood came out of the winepress, even unto the horse bridles, by the space of a thousand and six hundred furlongs.
text.NABRE: The wine press was trodden outside the city and blood poured out of the wine press to the height of a horse’s bridle for two hundred miles.
text.DRB: and the press was trodden without the city, and blood came forth out of the press, up to the horse bridles, for a thousand six hundred furlongs.

text.NRSV: Then I saw another portent in heaven, great and amazing: seven angels with seven plagues, which are the last, for with them the wrath of God is ended.
text.Rotherham: And I saw another sign in heaven, great and marvellous,—seven messengers having seven plagues, the last, because, in them, was ended the wrath of God.
text.KJV: And I saw another sign in heaven, great and marvellous, seven angels having the seven last plagues; for in them is filled up the wrath of God.
text.NABRE: Chapter 15 The Seven Last Plagues. Then I saw in heaven another sign, great and awe-inspiring: seven angels with the seven last plagues, for through them God’s fury is accomplished.
text.DRB: And I saw another sign in heaven great & marvelous: seven Angels having the seven last plagues. Because in them the wrath of God is consummate.

text.NRSV: And I saw what appeared to be a sea of glass mixed with fire, and those who had conquered the beast and its image and the number of its name, standing beside the sea of glass with harps of God in their hands.
text.Rotherham: And I saw as a glassy sea mingled with fire, and them who escape victorious from the beast, and from his image, and from the number of his name, standing upon the glassy sea, having harps of God;
text.KJV: And I saw as it were a sea of glass mingled with fire: and them that had gotten the victory over the beast, and over his image, and over his mark, and over the number of his name, stand on the sea of glass, having the harps of God.
text.NABRE: Then I saw something like a sea of glass mingled with fire. On the sea of glass were standing those who had won the victory over the beast and its image and the number that signified its name. They were holding God’s harps,
text.DRB: And I saw as it were a sea of glass mingled with fire, & them that overcame the beast and his image and the number of his name, standing upon the sea of glass, having the harps of God:

text.NRSV: And they sing the song of Moses, the servant of God, and the song of the Lamb: “Great and amazing are your deeds, Lord God the Almighty! Just and true are your ways, King of the nations!
text.Rotherham: and they sing the song of Moses the servant of God and the song of the Lamb, saying—Great and marvelous, are thy works, Lord, God, the Almighty! Righteous and true, are thy ways, O King of the ages!
text.KJV: And they sing the song of Moses the servant of God, and the song of the Lamb, saying, Great and marvellous are thy works, Lord God Almighty; just and true are thy ways, thou King of saints.
text.NABRE: and they sang the song of Moses, the servant of God, and the song of the Lamb: “Great and wonderful are your works, Lord God almighty. Just and true are your ways, O king of the nations.
text.DRB: and singing the song of Moyses the servant of God, and the song of the Lamb, saying: Great and marvelous are thy works Lord God omnipotent: just and true are thy ways King of the worlds.

text.NRSV: Lord, who will not fear and glorify your name? For you alone are holy. All nations will come and worship before you, for your judgments have been revealed.”
text.Rotherham: Who shall in anywise not be put in fear, O Lord, and glorify thy name,—because, alone, full of lovingkindness; because, all the nations, will have come, and will do homage before thee, because, thy righteous deeds, were made manifest?
text.KJV: Who shall not fear thee, O Lord, and glorify thy name? for thou only art holy: for all nations shall come and worship before thee; for thy judgments are made manifest.
text.NABRE: Who will not fear you, Lord, or glorify your name? For you alone are holy. All the nations will come and worship before you, for your righteous acts have been revealed.”
text.DRB: Who shall not fear thee, o Lord, and magnify thy name? Because thou only art holy, because all nations shall come, and adore in thy sight, because thy judgements be manifest.

text.NRSV: After this I looked, and the temple of the tent of witness in heaven was opened,
text.Rotherham: And, after these things, I saw, and the sanctuary of The Tent of Witness in heaven, was opened;
text.KJV: And after that I looked, and, behold, the temple of the tabernacle of the testimony in heaven was opened:
text.NABRE: After this I had another vision. The temple that is the heavenly tent of testimony opened,
text.DRB: And after these things I looked, and behold the temple of the tabernacle of testimony was opened in heaven:

text.NRSV: and out of the temple came the seven angels with the seven plagues, robed in pure bright linen, with golden sashes across their chests.
text.Rotherham: And the seven messengers who had the seven plagues, came forth, out of the sanctuary clothed with a [precious] stone, pure, bright, and girt about the breasts with girdles of gold.
text.KJV: And the seven angels came out of the temple, having the seven plagues, clothed in pure and white linen, and having their breasts girded with golden girdles.
text.NABRE: and the seven angels with the seven plagues came out of the temple. They were dressed in clean white linen, with a gold sash around their chests.
text.DRB: and there issued forth the seven Angels, having the seven plagues, from the temple: revested with clean and white stone, and girded about the breasts with girdles of gold.

text.NRSV: Then one of the four living creatures gave the seven angels seven golden bowls full of the wrath of God, who lives forever and ever;
text.Rotherham: And, one of the four living creatures, gave, unto the seven messengers, seven golden bowls, full of the wrath of God who liveth unto the ages of ages.
text.KJV: And one of the four beasts gave unto the seven angels seven golden vials full of the wrath of God, who liveth for ever and ever.
text.NABRE: One of the four living creatures gave the seven angels seven gold bowls filled with the fury of God, who lives forever and ever.
text.DRB: And one of the four beasts, gave to the seven Angels seven vials of gold full of the wrath of the God that liveth for ever and ever.

text.NRSV: and the temple was filled with smoke from the glory of God and from his power, and no one could enter the temple until the seven plagues of the seven angels were ended.
text.Rotherham: And the sanctuary was filled with smoke by reason of the glory of God, and by reason of his power; and, no one, was able to enter into the sanctuary, until the seven plagues of the seven messengers should be ended.
text.KJV: And the temple was filled with smoke from the glory of God, and from his power; and no man was able to enter into the temple, till the seven plagues of the seven angels were fulfilled.
text.NABRE: Then the temple became so filled with the smoke from God’s glory and might that no one could enter it until the seven plagues of the seven angels had been accomplished.
text.DRB: And the temple was filled with smoke at the majesty of God, and at his power: and no man could enter into the temple, till the seven plagues of the seven Angels were consummate.

text.NRSV: Then I heard a loud voice from the temple telling the seven angels, “Go and pour out on the earth the seven bowls of the wrath of God.”
text.Rotherham: And I heard aloud voice, out of the sanctuary, saying unto the seven messengers—Go, and be pouring out the seven bowls of the wrath of God unto the earth.
text.KJV: And I heard a great voice out of the temple saying to the seven angels, Go your ways, and pour out the vials of the wrath of God upon the earth.
text.NABRE: Chapter 16 The Seven Bowls. I heard a loud voice speaking from the temple to the seven angels, “Go and pour out the seven bowls of God’s fury upon the earth.”
text.DRB: And I heard a great voice out of the temple, saying to the seven Angels: Go, and pour out the seven vials of the wrath of God upon the earth.

text.NRSV: So the first angel went and poured his bowl on the earth, and a foul and painful sore came on those who had the mark of the beast and who worshiped its image.
text.Rotherham: And the first departed, and poured out his bowl unto the earth; and there came to be a baneful and painful ulcer, upon the men who had the mark of the beast, and them who were doing homage unto his image.
text.KJV: And the first went, and poured out his vial upon the earth; and there fell a noisome and grievous sore upon the men which had the mark of the beast, and upon them which worshipped his image.
text.NABRE: The first angel went and poured out his bowl on the earth. Festering and ugly sores broke out on those who had the mark of the beast or worshiped its image.
text.DRB: And the first went, and poured out his vial upon the earth, & there was made a cruel & very sore wound upon men that had the character of the beast: and upon them that adored the image thereof.

text.NRSV: The second angel poured his bowl into the sea, and it became like the blood of a corpse, and every living thing in the sea died.
text.Rotherham: And, the second, poured out his bowl into the sea; and it became blood, as of a dead man, and, every living soul, died—as regardeth the things in the sea.
text.KJV: And the second angel poured out his vial upon the sea; and it became as the blood of a dead man: and every living soul died in the sea.
text.NABRE: The second angel poured out his bowl on the sea. The sea turned to blood like that from a corpse; every creature living in the sea died.
text.DRB: And the second Angel poured out his vial upon the sea, and there was made blood as it were of one dead: and every living soul died in the sea.

text.NRSV: The third angel poured his bowl into the rivers and the fountains of water, and they became blood.
text.Rotherham: And, the third, poured out his bowl into the rivers, and the fountains of waters; and they became blood.
text.KJV: And the third angel poured out his vial upon the rivers and fountains of waters; and they became blood.
text.NABRE: The third angel poured out his bowl on the rivers and springs of water. These also turned to blood.
text.DRB: And the third poured out his vial upon the rivers and the fountains of waters: and there was made blood.

text.NRSV: And I heard the angel of the waters say, “You are just, O Holy One, who are and were, for you have judged these things;
text.Rotherham: And I heard the messenger of the waters, saying—Righteous, art thou Who art, and Who wast, Who art full of lovingkindness,—in that, these things, thou hast adjudged;
text.KJV: And I heard the angel of the waters say, Thou art righteous, O Lord, which art, and wast, and shalt be, because thou hast judged thus.
text.NABRE: Then I heard the angel in charge of the waters say: “You are just, O Holy One, who are and who were, in passing this sentence.
text.DRB: And I heard the Angel of the waters, saying: Thou art just, o Lord, which art, and which wast, the holy one, because thou hast judged these things:

text.NRSV: because they shed the blood of saints and prophets, you have given them blood to drink. It is what they deserve!”
text.Rotherham: Because, blood of saints and prophets, poured they out, and, blood, unto them, hast thou given to drink: Worthy, they are!
text.KJV: For they have shed the blood of saints and prophets, and thou hast given them blood to drink; for they are worthy.
text.NABRE: For they have shed the blood of the holy ones and the prophets, and you [have] given them blood to drink; it is what they deserve.”
text.DRB: because they have shed the blood of the Saints and Prophets, & thou hast given them blood to drink. For they are worthy.

text.NRSV: And I heard the altar respond, “Yes, O Lord God, the Almighty, your judgments are true and just!”
text.Rotherham: And I heard the altar, saying—Yea! Lord, God, the Almighty: True and righteous, are thy judgments!
text.KJV: And I heard another out of the altar say, Even so, Lord God Almighty, true and righteous are thy judgments.
text.NABRE: Then I heard the altar cry out, “Yes, Lord God almighty, your judgments are true and just.”
text.DRB: And I heard another, saying: Yea Lord God omnipotent, true and just are thy judgements.

text.NRSV: The fourth angel poured his bowl on the sun, and it was allowed to scorch people with fire;
text.Rotherham: And, the fourth, poured out his bowl upon the sun; and it was given unto it, to scorch mankind with fire;
text.KJV: And the fourth angel poured out his vial upon the sun; and power was given unto him to scorch men with fire.
text.NABRE: The fourth angel poured out his bowl on the sun. It was given the power to burn people with fire.
text.DRB: And the fourth Angel poured out his vial upon the sun, and it was given unto him to afflict men with heat and fire:

text.NRSV: they were scorched by the fierce heat, but they cursed the name of God who had authority over these plagues, and they did not repent and give him glory.
text.Rotherham: and mankind were scorched with a great scorching heat, and they blasphemed the name of God who had authority over these plagues, and repented not to give him glory.
text.KJV: And men were scorched with great heat, and blasphemed the name of God, which hath power over these plagues: and they repented not to give him glory.
text.NABRE: People were burned by the scorching heat and blasphemed the name of God who had power over these plagues, but they did not repent or give him glory.
text.DRB: and men boiled with great heat, and blasphemed the name of God having power over these plagues, neither did they penance to give him glory.

text.NRSV: The fifth angel poured his bowl on the throne of the beast, and its kingdom was plunged into darkness; people gnawed their tongues in agony,
text.Rotherham: And, the fifth, poured out his bowl upon the throne of the beast; and his kingdom became darkened, and they began to gnaw their tongues by reason of the pain,—
text.KJV: And the fifth angel poured out his vial upon the seat of the beast; and his kingdom was full of darkness; and they gnawed their tongues for pain,
text.NABRE: The fifth angel poured out his bowl on the throne of the beast. Its kingdom was plunged into darkness, and people bit their tongues in pain
text.DRB: And the fifth Angel poured out his vial upon the seat of the beast: and his kingdom was made dark, and they together did eat their tongues for pain:

text.NRSV: and cursed the God of heaven because of their pains and sores, and they did not repent of their deeds.
text.Rotherham: and they blasphemed the God of heaven, by reason of their pain, and by reason of their ulcers, and repented not of their works.
text.KJV: And blasphemed the God of heaven because of their pains and their sores, and repented not of their deeds.
text.NABRE: and blasphemed the God of heaven because of their pains and sores. But they did not repent of their works.
text.DRB: and they blasphemed the God of heaven because of their pains and wounds, and did not penance from their works.

text.NRSV: The sixth angel poured his bowl on the great river Euphrates, and its water was dried up in order to prepare the way for the kings from the east.
text.Rotherham: And, the sixth, poured out his bowl upon the great river: [the] Euphrates; and the water thereof, was dried up, that the way might be prepared, of the kings who were from the rising of the sun.
text.KJV: And the sixth angel poured out his vial upon the great river Euphrates; and the water thereof was dried up, that the way of the kings of the east might be prepared.
text.NABRE: The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great river Euphrates. Its water was dried up to prepare the way for the kings of the East.
text.DRB: And the sixth Angel poured out his vial upon that great river Euphrates: and dried up the water thereof that a way might be prepared to the kings from the rising of the sun.

text.NRSV: And I saw three foul spirits like frogs coming from the mouth of the dragon, from the mouth of the beast, and from the mouth of the false prophet.
text.Rotherham: And I saw, out of the mouth of the dragon, and out of the mouth of the beast, and out of the mouth of the false-prophet, three impure spirits, as frogs;
text.KJV: And I saw three unclean spirits like frogs come out of the mouth of the dragon, and out of the mouth of the beast, and out of the mouth of the false prophet.
text.NABRE: I saw three unclean spirits like frogs come from the mouth of the dragon, from the mouth of the beast, and from the mouth of the false prophet.
text.DRB: And I saw from the mouth of the dragon, and from the mouth of the beast, and from the mouth of the false-prophet three unclean spirits in manner of frogs.

text.NRSV: These are demonic spirits, performing signs, who go abroad to the kings of the whole world, to assemble them for battle on the great day of God the Almighty.
text.Rotherham: for they are spirits of demons, doing signs, which are to go forth unto the kings of the whole habitable earth, to gather them together unto the battle of the great day of God the Almighty.—
text.KJV: For they are the spirits of devils, working miracles, which go forth unto the kings of the earth and of the whole world, to gather them to the battle of that great day of God Almighty.
text.NABRE: These were demonic spirits who performed signs. They went out to the kings of the whole world to assemble them for the battle on the great day of God the almighty.
text.DRB: For they are the spirits of devils working signs, and they go forth to the kings of the whole earth to gather them into battle at the great day of the omnipotent God.

text.NRSV: (“See, I am coming like a thief! Blessed is the one who stays awake and is clothed, not going about naked and exposed to shame.”)
text.Rotherham: Lo! I come as a thief! Happy, he that is watching, and keeping his garments, lest, naked, he be walking, and they see his shame.—
text.KJV: Behold, I come as a thief. Blessed is he that watcheth, and keepeth his garments, lest he walk naked, and they see his shame.
text.NABRE: (“Behold, I am coming like a thief.” Blessed is the one who watches and keeps his clothes ready, so that he may not go naked and people see him exposed.)
text.DRB: Behold I come as a thief: Blessed is he that watcheth, & keepeth his garments, that he walk not naked, and they see his turpitude.

text.NRSV: And they assembled them at the place that in Hebrew is called Harmagedon.
text.Rotherham: And he gathered them together unto the place that is called, in Hebrew, Har Magedon.
text.KJV: And he gathered them together into a place called in the Hebrew tongue Armageddon.
text.NABRE: They then assembled the kings in the place that is named Armageddon in Hebrew.
text.DRB: And he shall gather them into a place which in Hebrew is called Armagedon.

text.NRSV: The seventh angel poured his bowl into the air, and a loud voice came out of the temple, from the throne, saying, “It is done!”
text.Rotherham: And, the seventh, poured out his bowl upon the air.—And there came forth a loud voice out of the sanctuary, from the throne, saying—Accomplished!
text.KJV: And the seventh angel poured out his vial into the air; and there came a great voice out of the temple of heaven, from the throne, saying, It is done.
text.NABRE: The seventh angel poured out his bowl into the air. A loud voice came out of the temple from the throne, saying, “It is done.”
text.DRB: And the seventh Angel poured out his vial upon the air, and there came forth a loud voice out of the temple from the throne, saying: It is done.

text.NRSV: And there were flashes of lightning, rumblings, peals of thunder, and a violent earthquake, such as had not occurred since people were upon the earth, so violent was that earthquake.
text.Rotherham: And there came to be lightnings, and voices, and thunders; and, a great earthquake, took place,—such as had never taken place since men came to be on the earth,—such a mighty earthquake, so great;
text.KJV: And there were voices, and thunders, and lightnings; and there was a great earthquake, such as was not since men were upon the earth, so mighty an earthquake, and so great.
text.NABRE: Then there were lightning flashes, rumblings, and peals of thunder, and a great earthquake. It was such a violent earthquake that there has never been one like it since the human race began on earth.
text.DRB: And there were made lightnings, and voices, and thunders, and a great earth-quake was made, such an one as never hath been since men were upon the earth, such an earth-quake, so great.

text.NRSV: The great city was split into three parts, and the cities of the nations fell. God remembered great Babylon and gave her the cup of the wine of the fury of his wrath.
text.Rotherham: and the great city became [divided] into three parts, and the cities of the nations fell; and, Babylon the Great, was brought into remembrance before God, to give unto her the cup of the wine of the wrath of his anger;
text.KJV: And the great city was divided into three parts, and the cities of the nations fell: and great Babylon came in remembrance before God, to give unto her the cup of the wine of the fierceness of his wrath.
text.NABRE: The great city was split into three parts, and the gentile cities fell. But God remembered great Babylon, giving it the cup filled with the wine of his fury and wrath.
text.DRB: And the great city was made into three parts: and the cities of the Gentiles fell. And Babylon the great came into memory before God, to give her the cup of wine of the indignation of his wrath.

text.NRSV: And every island fled away, and no mountains were to be found;
text.Rotherham: and, every island, fled, and, mountains, were not found.
text.KJV: And every island fled away, and the mountains were not found.
text.NABRE: Every island fled, and mountains disappeared.
text.DRB: And every Island fled, and mountains were not found.

text.NRSV: and huge hailstones, each weighing about a hundred pounds, dropped from heaven on people, until they cursed God for the plague of the hail, so fearful was that plague.
text.Rotherham: And, great hail, as talents, cometh down out of heaven upon mankind; and men blasphemed God, by reason of the plague of hail,—because the plague thereof was, exceeding great.
text.KJV: And there fell upon men a great hail out of heaven, every stone about the weight of a talent: and men blasphemed God because of the plague of the hail; for the plague thereof was exceeding great.
text.NABRE: Large hailstones like huge weights came down from the sky on people, and they blasphemed God for the plague of hail because this plague was so severe.
text.DRB: And great hail like a talent came down from heaven upon men: and men blasphemed God for the plague of the hail: because it was made exceeding great.

text.NRSV: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls came and said to me, “Come, I will show you the judgment of the great whore who is seated on many waters,
text.Rotherham: And one of the seven messengers who had the seven bowls came, and spake with me, saying—Hither! I will point out to thee the judgment of the great harlot, who sitteth upon many waters,
text.KJV: And there came one of the seven angels which had the seven vials, and talked with me, saying unto me, Come hither; I will shew unto thee the judgment of the great whore that sitteth upon many waters:
text.NABRE: V. The Punishment of Babylon and the Destruction of Pagan Nations Chapter 17 Babylon the Great. Then one of the seven angels who were holding the seven bowls came and said to me, “Come here. I will show you the judgment on the great harlot who lives near the many waters.
text.DRB: And there came one of the seven Angels which had the seven vials, & spake with me, saying: Come, I will shew thee the damnation of the great harlot, which sitteth upon many waters.

text.NRSV: with whom the kings of the earth have committed fornication, and with the wine of whose fornication the inhabitants of the earth have become drunk.”
text.Rotherham: with whom the kings of the earth committed lewdness,—and they who were dwelling upon the earth were made drunk with the wine of her lewdness.
text.KJV: With whom the kings of the earth have committed fornication, and the inhabitants of the earth have been made drunk with the wine of her fornication.
text.NABRE: The kings of the earth have had intercourse with her, and the inhabitants of the earth became drunk on the wine of her harlotry.”
text.DRB: with whom the kings of the earth have fornicated, & they which inhabit the earth have been drunk of the wine of her whoredom.

text.NRSV: So he carried me away in the spirit into a wilderness, and I saw a woman sitting on a scarlet beast that was full of blasphemous names, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
text.Rotherham: And he carried me away into a desert, in spirit. And I saw a woman, sitting upon a scarlet wild-beast full of names of blasphemy, having seven heads and ten horns.
text.KJV: So he carried me away in the spirit into the wilderness: and I saw a woman sit upon a scarlet coloured beast, full of names of blasphemy, having seven heads and ten horns.
text.NABRE: Then he carried me away in spirit to a deserted place where I saw a woman seated on a scarlet beast that was covered with blasphemous names, with seven heads and ten horns.
text.DRB: And he took me away in spirit into the desert. And I saw a woman sitting upon a scarlet coloured beast, full of names of blasphemy, having seven heads, and ten horns.

text.NRSV: The woman was clothed in purple and scarlet, and adorned with gold and jewels and pearls, holding in her hand a golden cup full of abominations and the impurities of her fornication;
text.Rotherham: And, the woman, was arrayed with purple and scarlet, and decked with gold and precious stone and pearls,—having a cup of gold in her hand, full of abominations and the impurities of her lewdness;
text.KJV: And the woman was arrayed in purple and scarlet colour, and decked with gold and precious stones and pearls, having a golden cup in her hand full of abominations and filthiness of her fornication:
text.NABRE: The woman was wearing purple and scarlet and adorned with gold, precious stones, and pearls. She held in her hand a gold cup that was filled with the abominable and sordid deeds of her harlotry.
text.DRB: And the woman was clothed round about with purple and scarlet, and gilted with gold, and precious stone, and pearls, having a golden cup in her hand, full of the abomination & filthiness of her fornication.

text.NRSV: and on her forehead was written a name, a mystery: “Babylon the great, mother of whores and of earth’s abominations.”
text.Rotherham: and, upon her forehead, a name written, a secret: Babylon the great, the Mother of the Harlots and of the Abominations of the earth.
text.KJV: And upon her forehead was a name written, MYSTERY, BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS AND ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH.
text.NABRE: On her forehead was written a name, which is a mystery, “Babylon the great, the mother of harlots and of the abominations of the earth.”
text.DRB: And in her forehead a name written, Mystery: Babylon the great, mother of the fornications and the abominations of the earth.

text.NRSV: And I saw that the woman was drunk with the blood of the saints and the blood of the witnesses to Jesus. When I saw her, I was greatly amazed.
text.Rotherham: And I saw the woman, drunk with the blood of the saints, and with the blood of the witnesses of Jesus. And I was astonished, when I beheld her, with great astonishment.
text.KJV: And I saw the woman drunken with the blood of the saints, and with the blood of the martyrs of Jesus: and when I saw her, I wondered with great admiration.
text.NABRE: I saw that the woman was drunk on the blood of the holy ones and on the blood of the witnesses to Jesus. Meaning of the Beast and Harlot. When I saw her I was greatly amazed.
text.DRB: And I saw the woman drunken of the blood of Saints, and of the blood of the Martyrs of Jesus. And I marveled when I had seen her, with great admiration.

text.NRSV: But the angel said to me, “Why are you amazed? I will tell you the mystery of the woman, and of the beast with seven heads and ten horns that carries her.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger said unto me—Wherefore wast thou astonished? I, will tell thee the secret of the woman, and of the wild-beast that carrieth her, which hath the seven heads and the ten horns.
text.KJV: And the angel said unto me, Wherefore didst thou marvel? I will tell thee the mystery of the woman, and of the beast that carrieth her, which hath the seven heads and ten horns.
text.NABRE: The angel said to me, “Why are you amazed? I will explain to you the mystery of the woman and of the beast that carries her, the beast with the seven heads and the ten horns.
text.DRB: And the Angel said to me: Why doest thou marvel? I will tell thee the mystery of the woman, and of the beast that carrieth her, which hath the seven heads, and the ten horns.

text.NRSV: The beast that you saw was, and is not, and is about to ascend from the bottomless pit and go to destruction. And the inhabitants of the earth, whose names have not been written in the book of life from the foundation of the world, will be amazed when they see the beast, because it was and is not and is to come.
text.Rotherham: The wild-beast which thou sawest, was, and is not, and is about to come up out of the abyss, and into, destruction, goeth away. And they who are dwelling upon the earth whose name is not written upon the book of life from the foundation of the world, will be astonished, when they see the wild-beast, because it was, and is not, and shall be present.
text.KJV: The beast that thou sawest was, and is not; and shall ascend out of the bottomless pit, and go into perdition: and they that dwell on the earth shall wonder, whose names were not written in the book of life from the foundation of the world, when they behold the beast that was, and is not, and yet is.
text.NABRE: The beast that you saw existed once but now exists no longer. It will come up from the abyss and is headed for destruction. The inhabitants of the earth whose names have not been written in the book of life from the foundation of the world shall be amazed when they see the beast, because it existed once but exists no longer, and yet it will come again.
text.DRB: The beast which thou sawest, was, and is not, and shall come up out of the bottomless depth, and go into destruction: and the inhabitants on the earth (whose names are not written in the book of life from the making of the world) shall marvel, seeing the beast that was, and is not.

text.NRSV: “This calls for a mind that has wisdom: the seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman is seated; also, they are seven kings,
text.Rotherham: Here, is the mind that hath wisdom. The seven heads, are, seven mountains, whereupon the woman sitteth;
text.KJV: And here is the mind which hath wisdom. The seven heads are seven mountains, on which the woman sitteth.
text.NABRE: Here is a clue for one who has wisdom. The seven heads represent seven hills upon which the woman sits. They also represent seven kings:
text.DRB: And here is understanding, that hath wisdom. The seven heads, are seven hills, upon which the woman sitteth, and they are seven kings.

text.NRSV: of whom five have fallen, one is living, and the other has not yet come; and when he comes, he must remain only a little while.
text.Rotherham: and they are, seven kings: the five, have fallen, the one, is, the other, hath not yet come; and, whensoever he shall come, a little while, must he remain,
text.KJV: And there are seven kings: five are fallen, and one is, and the other is not yet come; and when he cometh, he must continue a short space.
text.NABRE: five have already fallen, one still lives, and the last has not yet come, and when he comes he must remain only a short while.
text.DRB: Five are fallen, one is, and another is not yet come: and when he shall come, he must tarry a short time.

text.NRSV: As for the beast that was and is not, it is an eighth but it belongs to the seven, and it goes to destruction.
text.Rotherham: and the wild-beast which was and is not. And he, is an eighth, and is, of the seven,—and, into destruction, goeth away.
text.KJV: And the beast that was, and is not, even he is the eighth, and is of the seven, and goeth into perdition.
text.NABRE: The beast that existed once but exists no longer is an eighth king, but really belongs to the seven and is headed for destruction.
text.DRB: And the beast which was, and is not: the same also is the eight, and is of the seven, & goeth into destruction.

text.NRSV: And the ten horns that you saw are ten kings who have not yet received a kingdom, but they are to receive authority as kings for one hour, together with the beast.
text.Rotherham: And, the ten horns which thou sawest, are, ten kings,—who, indeed, have not received, sovereignty, as yet, but, authority, as kings, for one hour, shall receive, with the wild-beast.
text.KJV: And the ten horns which thou sawest are ten kings, which have received no kingdom as yet; but receive power as kings one hour with the beast.
text.NABRE: The ten horns that you saw represent ten kings who have not yet been crowned; they will receive royal authority along with the beast for one hour.
